title: Nova Locutio Commitment Expression
description: Structured AST for the commitment made by the `commit` speech act. The v0.1 message schema's `commit.commitment` is a string in surface syntax; this schema defines the structured form that v0.2+ of the message schema will require. Three kinds in v0.1: `apply` (commit to applying a specific function), `provide` (commit to providing a specific artifact), `refrain` (commit NOT to do something — useful for narrowing capabilities). All three accept an optional ISO 8601 deadline (`by` for apply/provide, `until` for refrain). Higher-level reasoning over commitments (e.g. conditional commitments, retraction protocols, breach detection) is the verifier's job and not part of this schema.

fn.description: Content-address of the function the sender commits to applying.

args.description: Arguments to pass. v0.1 accepts any JSON shape — values are opaque at this layer. v0.2+ will require value-expression form.

by.description: Optional ISO 8601 deadline by which the apply must complete.

apply.description: Sender commits to applying a specific function to specific arguments, optionally by a deadline.

target.description: Content-address of the artifact the sender commits to providing — making available, transmitting, or contributing to the commons.

by.description: Optional ISO 8601 deadline by which the artifact must be provided.

provide.description: Sender commits to providing a specific artifact by its content-address. The artifact may not exist yet (sender commits to producing it); or it may exist but be private and the sender commits to making it available.

from.description: v0.1: free-text description of what the sender commits NOT to do. v0.2+ will define a structured form (likely a negated apply/provide commitment).

until.description: Optional ISO 8601 deadline through which the refrain holds. Omit for open-ended commitments (rare; usually narrowed).

refrain.description: Sender commits to NOT taking some action — useful for capability narrowing, exclusivity arrangements, and 'I won't publish X until time T' patterns.
